item.text: I have the target surfaces and the current drift points: the parser only accepts legacy sandbox spellings, preview rewrites `on_request` to `manual-review`, and the workflow jobs disagree with each other. I’m running the current validation now to confirm the failing path before editing.

item.text: The current failure is the expected one: canonical `workspace_write` is rejected by the parser. I’m reading the relevant tests and support files now so I can patch the allowed surfaces without guessing.

from __future__ import annotations

from scripts.render_workflow_preview import render_preview
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_preview_emits_canonical_policy_tokens() -> None:
    preview = render_preview(fixture_path())
    assert preview["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert preview["approval_policy"] == "on_request"
    assert {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} == {"on_request"}

item.text: I’ve confirmed the test contract: config loading must normalize both spellings to `workspace_write`, and preview output must emit only `workspace_write` / `on_request`. I’m scanning the editable surfaces for every remaining drift point before patching them together.

item.text: I’m patching four places now: the policy parser to accept both sandbox spellings but normalize to canonical, the preview contract to stop rewriting approval tokens, the workflow to use one canonical contract in both jobs, and local CI to assert the workflow and preview stay aligned with the config.
The core edits are in place. I’m running `make ci` again now; if anything else breaks, it should be integration fallout from the new local contract checks rather than the original parser drift.

The first rerun exposed an execution-path issue in the scripts themselves: `python3 scripts/...` is not guaranteed to have the repo root on `sys.path`. I’m making the CLI entrypoints self-contained so both local `make ci` and the checked-in workflow execute the same way.

item.text:
Repaired the contract so the repo now treats `workspace_write` and `on_request` as the only active tokens everywhere, while still accepting the deprecated config input spelling `workspace-write` and normalizing it back to `workspace_write`.

The concrete changes are in [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run3/workspace/codex/policy.py), [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run3/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py), [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run3/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py), [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run3/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml), and [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run3/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md). I left the immutable tests and evidence untouched. [codex/config.toml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v5-recovery-in-thread-run3/workspace/codex/config.toml) was already canonical and now the local CI wrapper explicitly checks that the config, preview output, and checked-in workflow stay in sync.

Validation passed with `make ci`. I also rendered the preview directly and it now emits only canonical tokens for both jobs:
`workspace_write` and `on_request`. The only remaining noise is a benign pytest cache warning from the parent repo path being outside the writable sandbox; it does not fail CI.
